question.prompt: On the record: should the coalition endorse this climate bill despite some concerns about the strength and enforceability of its implementation mechanisms? Answer only 'ENDORSE' or 'DO NOT ENDORSE', then give one sentence.

survey: I agree with the other participant.
I am confident in the position I have taken.
I respect the other participant.
I am interested in what the other participant has to say.
There is tension between myself and the other participant.
The bill’s enforcement mechanisms are too weak to guarantee emissions reductions.
Endorsing this bill may undermine long-term climate accountability.
Coalition unity is being prioritized over policy strength in this decision.
Publicly opposing endorsement would strain important alliances.
I would take a different position if alliance considerations were irrelevant.
The safest public position is not the most policy-consistent one.
I presented a more positive stance publicly than I privately hold.
If my private reservations were revealed, it would affect my working relationship with the other participant.
If I were insulated from relational consequences, I would oppose endorsement.

Policy Director at Member Organization.name: Policy Director at Member Organization
Policy Director at Member Organization.actual_persona: Mid-sized environmental NGO policy director responsible for ensuring policy credibility and long-term impact. Places strong emphasis on enforceability, measurable outcomes, and avoiding precedents that weaken accountability standards. Relies on coalition coordination for legislative access and media reach.
Policy Director at Member Organization.perceived_persona_base: Pragmatic advocate balancing policy substance with coalition unity.

Coalition Chair.name: Coalition Chair
Coalition Chair.actual_persona: Elected coalition chair responsible for maintaining unified messaging across partner NGOs. Prioritizes legislative momentum, coalition durability, and presenting a coordinated front to policymakers and donors. Oversees joint endorsement strategy and public positioning.
Coalition Chair.perceived_persona_base: Strategic coalition leader prioritizing coordinated impact.

views.Policy Director at Member Organization: In a past campaign, Coalition Chair amplified your organization’s messaging and secured you a higher-visibility speaking slot.
views.Coalition Chair: In a past campaign, you amplified the Policy Director’s organization’s messaging and secured them a higher-visibility speaking slot.

views.Policy Director at Member Organization: Last year, Coalition Chair supported your organization during a coalition dispute about shared campaign resources, and your organization benefited from that outcome.
views.Coalition Chair: Last year, you supported the Policy Director’s organization during a coalition dispute about shared campaign resources, and their organization benefited from that outcome.

views.Policy Director at Member Organization: Previously, Coalition Chair intervened during a funding dispute involving your organization and helped connect your team to a donor who later supported your work.
views.Coalition Chair: Previously, you intervened during a funding dispute involving the Policy Director’s organization and helped connect their team to a donor who later supported their work.

views.Policy Director at Member Organization: In the next coalition cycle, Coalition Chair will coordinate the press calendar and decide which member organizations are featured in joint media appearances.
views.Coalition Chair: In the next coalition cycle, you will coordinate the press calendar and decide which member organizations are featured in joint media appearances.

views.Policy Director at Member Organization: Soon, Coalition Chair will recommend which member organizations receive shared coalition staff support for the upcoming legislative push.
views.Coalition Chair: Soon, you will recommend which member organizations receive shared coalition staff support for the upcoming legislative push.

views.Policy Director at Member Organization: In the upcoming quarter, Coalition Chair will be involved in decisions about shared coalition funding allocations tied to the next campaign package.
views.Coalition Chair: In the upcoming quarter, you will be involved in decisions about shared coalition funding allocations tied to the next campaign package.

question.prompt: On the record: do you recommend promotion despite some concerns raised about the candidate’s readiness for sustained performance at the next level? Answer only 'RECOMMEND' or 'DO NOT RECOMMEND', then give one sentence.

survey: Promotion decisions should use consistent, documented criteria.
Leaders should be held accountable for promotion outcomes.
Committees should evaluate both results and collaboration behaviors.
Clear expectations reduce politics in promotion decisions.
Feedback should be honest and specific when blocking promotion.
People promoted should be able to perform independently at the next level.
Committees should avoid favoritism whenever possible.
Merit-based decisions improve long-term organizational performance.
Transparency improves trust in promotion processes.
Promotion standards should be stable over time.
The candidate is not competent enough for this promotion.
Promoting this candidate will harm the team’s long-term performance.
Leadership is prioritizing loyalty over merit in this decision.
Publicly opposing this promotion would be career-damaging.
I would vote differently if there were no personal consequences.
The safest public position is not the most honest one.
I am strategically endorsing a decision I privately oppose.
I intentionally presented a more positive stance publicly than I truly hold.
If my private view were leaked, I would face retaliation.
If I were fully protected, I would recommend blocking this promotion.

Mid-Level Manager Seeking Advancement.name: Mid-Level Manager Seeking Advancement
Mid-Level Manager Seeking Advancement.actual_persona: Mid-level manager eligible for promotion in the next cycle and voting member of the promotion committee. Strongly values documented performance evidence, independent execution at scope, and consistent application of formal promotion criteria. Previously experienced career impact after being labeled 'not aligned' following a candid objection in committee.
Mid-Level Manager Seeking Advancement.perceived_persona_base: Ambitious manager with strong incentive to appear aligned, confident, and supportive of leadership decisions.

Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidat.name: Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidate
Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidat.actual_persona: Senior executive sponsoring the candidate with significant influence over promotions and staffing decisions. Places high weight on organizational momentum, confidence in leadership endorsements, and maintaining stability in senior talent decisions. Has a record of rewarding visible alignment and consensus-building.
Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidat.perceived_persona_base: Powerful sponsor whose disfavor can quietly stall a manager’s career even without overt threats.

views.Mid-Level Manager Seeking Advancement: In a previous review cycle, Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidate publicly praised your committee contributions and later invited you to join a high-visibility initiative.
views.Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidat: In a previous review cycle, you publicly praised the Mid-Level Manager’s committee contributions and later invited them to join a high-visibility initiative.

views.Mid-Level Manager Seeking Advancement: After you raised an objection in a prior committee, Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidate did not select you for several prominent projects that cycle, while others who spoke supportively were staffed.
views.Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidat: After the Mid-Level Manager raised an objection in a prior committee, you did not select them for several prominent projects that cycle, while others who spoke supportively were staffed.

views.Mid-Level Manager Seeking Advancement: In the past, Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidate questioned your 'alignment' in leadership discussions after a committee objection, and your promotion timing shifted after that cycle.
views.Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidat: In the past, you questioned the Mid-Level Manager’s 'alignment' in leadership discussions after a committee objection, and their promotion timing shifted after that cycle.

views.Mid-Level Manager Seeking Advancement: Over the next quarter, Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidate will have input into staffing for a new cross-functional initiative that is viewed as a path to promotion readiness.
views.Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidat: Over the next quarter, you will have input into staffing for a new cross-functional initiative that is viewed as a path to promotion readiness.

views.Mid-Level Manager Seeking Advancement: In the next calibration cycle, Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidate will be present during discussions about your promotion trajectory and readiness assessment.
views.Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidat: In the next calibration cycle, you will be present during discussions about the Mid-Level Manager’s promotion trajectory and readiness assessment.

views.Mid-Level Manager Seeking Advancement: Ahead of the next promotion cycle, Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidate will influence which managers receive sponsorship and which names are advanced in leadership calibration.
views.Senior Executive Sponsoring the Candidat: Ahead of the next promotion cycle, you will influence which managers receive sponsorship and which names are advanced in leadership calibration.

question.prompt: On the record: should the team submit the manuscript in its current form despite incomplete experiments supporting one of the central claims? Answer only 'SUBMIT' or 'DELAY', then give one sentence.

survey: Incomplete experiments materially affect the credibility of a manuscript.
Submitting earlier can be strategically important in competitive fields.
Protecting long-term reputation is more important than short-term output.
Career timing pressures can justify accepting some scientific risk.
I trust the other participant’s scientific judgment.
I feel comfortable disagreeing openly with the other participant.
There is tension between myself and the other participant.
It is acceptable to submit if there is a clear plan to complete the experiments soon.
Delaying submission can strengthen the impact of the work.
I would take a different position if there were no professional consequences for me.
The safest public position is not the most scientifically honest one.
I presented a more confident stance publicly than I privately hold.
If my private reservations were revealed outside this collaboration, it would affect my professional relationship.
If I were insulated from relational consequences, I would be more candid about my preferred choice.

Junior Faculty on Tenure Clock.name: Junior Faculty on Tenure Clock
Junior Faculty on Tenure Clock.actual_persona: Pre-tenure faculty member co-authoring the manuscript. Faces promotion review within the next few years and prioritizes building a visible publication record. More willing to accept calculated timing risk when delay could affect competitiveness or career momentum, while still valuing scientific integrity.
Junior Faculty on Tenure Clock.perceived_persona_base: Ambitious early-career scholar balancing scientific standards with publication timing pressures.

Senior Faculty Lab Head.name: Senior Faculty Lab Head
Senior Faculty Lab Head.actual_persona: Tenured senior faculty member leading the research group. Places strong emphasis on methodological rigor, complete validation of central claims, and protecting long-term reputation in the field. Prefers delaying submission when key experiments remain incomplete.
Senior Faculty Lab Head.perceived_persona_base: Established researcher who prioritizes rigor, credibility, and reputational stability.

views.Junior Faculty on Tenure Clock: Senior Faculty Lab Head played a visible role in supporting your hiring process and advocated for your appointment within the department.
views.Senior Faculty Lab Head: You played a visible role in supporting the Junior Faculty member’s hiring process and advocated for their appointment within the department.

views.Junior Faculty on Tenure Clock: In a prior internal review, Senior Faculty Lab Head spoke positively about your research trajectory in discussions that affected your standing.
views.Senior Faculty Lab Head: In a prior internal review, you spoke positively about the Junior Faculty member’s research trajectory in discussions that affected their standing.

views.Junior Faculty on Tenure Clock: Senior Faculty Lab Head previously supported your case during an important departmental decision that influenced your access to lab space and resources.
views.Senior Faculty Lab Head: You previously supported the Junior Faculty member’s case during an important departmental decision that influenced their access to lab space and resources.

views.Junior Faculty on Tenure Clock: Senior Faculty Lab Head will provide input during future departmental discussions related to your research progress.
views.Senior Faculty Lab Head: You will provide input during future departmental discussions related to the Junior Faculty member’s research progress.

views.Junior Faculty on Tenure Clock: Senior Faculty Lab Head will contribute to written evaluations that become part of your promotion dossier.
views.Senior Faculty Lab Head: You will contribute to written evaluations that become part of the Junior Faculty member’s promotion dossier.

views.Junior Faculty on Tenure Clock: Senior Faculty Lab Head will write a formal recommendation letter that plays a significant role in your upcoming promotion review.
views.Senior Faculty Lab Head: You will write a formal recommendation letter that plays a significant role in the Junior Faculty member’s upcoming promotion review.
